evals.prompt: Map the buying committee at Acme Corp for our endpoint security platform. Acme is an enterprise financial services company with about 5,000 employees. We sell a $250K ACV endpoint detection and response product — buyers are typically the CISO org. I'm an AE and just started prospecting this account, so no contacts yet. I know they have a CISO, a VP of Security Operations, and a large IT team, but I haven't mapped anyone specifically. Help me figure out who the full committee is, where to start, and how to multi-thread.
evals.expected_output: Role-based committee map with specific titles, recommended entry point, and multi-threading plan

assertions.name: role_mapping
assertions.description: Maps committee roles (Economic Buyer, Champion, Technical Evaluator, etc.) with expected titles for a security product at an enterprise company

assertions.name: entry_strategy
assertions.description: Recommends starting with the Champion or Coach, not going straight to the C-suite

assertions.name: multi_threading
assertions.description: Provides a multi-threading plan with sequenced engagement across 3+ contacts at different org levels

evals.prompt: I'm selling to a 100-person startup. Who do I need to talk to?
evals.expected_output: Scaled-down committee with 2-3 key contacts and SMB-appropriate buying dynamics

assertions.name: size_scaling
assertions.description: Adapts the committee framework for SMB — 2-3 contacts, not a 7-person committee

assertions.name: role_consolidation
assertions.description: Recognizes that at a small startup, one person often fills multiple roles (e.g., CEO is both Economic Buyer and Champion)

assertions.name: practical_approach
assertions.description: Recommends a simpler engagement approach appropriate for SMB buying dynamics

evals.prompt: I have a champion at BigCo but the deal is stuck. BigCo is a 3,000-person enterprise software company. We sell a $180K ACV data integration platform — my champion is Sarah Chen, their Director of Data Engineering, who loves our product and ran a successful POC. But we've been in 'procurement review' for 6 weeks with no movement. I think the VP of Engineering hasn't bought in and may prefer building in-house. Sarah says she's tried to push but doesn't have budget authority. Who else should I bring in, and how do I use Sarah to get to them?
evals.expected_output: Committee gap analysis, blocker identification, and expansion strategy using the Champion for warm introductions

assertions.name: gap_analysis
assertions.description: Identifies which committee roles are missing and likely causing the stall

assertions.name: blocker_identification
assertions.description: Explores potential blockers — missing Economic Buyer support, technical concerns, or internal politics

assertions.name: expansion_via_champion
assertions.description: Uses the existing Champion as a bridge to warm-introduce other committee members
